description: Auto-activation rules for Claude Code skills - CITW + FOM merged system

standards.description: Standards enforcement system - auto-activates when modifying code files
standards.activationMessage: 📋 Standards skill activated. Review standards before writing code.

preWriteAction.description: Show relevant standards before modifying files

skill-developer.description: Meta-skill for creating and managing Claude Code skills

frontend-dev-guidelines.description: HTML/CSS/JavaScript best practices for static cruise planning site

seo-optimizer.description: DEPRECATED — superseded by ICP-2. SEO optimization that SUPPORTS ITW-Lite v3.010 philosophy: AI-first, Human-first, Google second. Focuses on technical SEO (schema.org, semantic HTML, meta tags) that benefits AI + humans + search engines simultaneously. MUST NOT suggest keyword stuffing, readability compromises, or removal of AI-helpful elements.

must_reject: Keyword stuffing or unnatural keyword density
Removing or discouraging AI-first meta tags (ai:summary, ai:*, content-protocol, etc)
must_accept: Schema.org structured data (Article, Place, TravelAction, Organization, etc)
Semantic HTML that helps AI comprehension AND accessibility
Meaningful alt text for accessibility AND SEO
guardrails.philosophy: Technical SEO that serves all three audiences: AI understanding, human experience, search discoverability

accessibility-auditor.description: Web accessibility compliance (WCAG AA) for cruise planning site

content-strategy.description: Content strategy for cruise planning content aligned with ITW-Lite v3.010 philosophy. Prioritizes authentic, natural language that serves humans first while maintaining AI comprehension. Travel storytelling over marketing-speak. Voice enforcement via Like-a-human skill.

must_reject: Unnatural, robotic copy optimized only for search
Removing planning guidance sections for brevity
must_accept: Planning guidance sections that help travelers make decisions
Authentic voice that prioritizes human reader
Structured content that AI can parse (headings, lists, schema)
guardrails.philosophy: Write for humans first. Make AI comprehension easy through structure, not through robotic language. Tell stories, don't just list features.

content-strategy.activationMessage: ✒️ Like-a-human v2.0.0 voice guide activated. Window Pane + Precision Discipline + Cadence in effect.

coActivation.note: ALL content edits (ports, ships, restaurants, logbooks, root pages) fire both Careful and Like-a-human. Careful governs HOW we work. Like-a-human governs HOW IT READS. Voice-audit fires before commits.

voice-audit.description: Post-draft diagnostic for content authenticity. Scans for machine tells, voice drift, hedging, promotional language, and emotional register flattening. Fires before committing content changes via voice-audit-hook.sh (PreToolUse on Bash matching git commit). Companion to Like-a-human (which fires during writing).

autoLoad.note: Loaded via voice-audit-hook.sh on PreToolUse Bash when git commit is detected with content files staged. Can also be loaded manually via prompt keywords.

voice-audit.activationMessage: 🔍 Voice audit diagnostic activated. Scanning for machine tells and voice drift.

coActivation.note: Voice-audit is the post-draft companion to Like-a-human. Like-a-human fires during writing. Voice-audit fires before committing.

emotional-hook-test.description: Pre-publication feeling-level quality gate. Evaluates reader emotional experience in 30 seconds: Clarity (0-5s), Calm (5-10s), Seen (10-15s), Confidence (15-20s), Guided (20-30s). Complements voice-audit (output quality) and Like-a-human (writing voice) with feeling-level assessment. Applied during content review.

autoLoad.note: Loaded manually via prompt keywords or co-activated with voice-audit during pre-commit content review. Not auto-loaded on every edit — it is a quality gate, not a writing guide.

emotional-hook-test.activationMessage: Emotional Hook Test activated. Evaluating reader feeling: Clarity → Calm → Seen → Confidence → Guided.

coActivation.note: Completes the content quality trio: Like-a-human (during writing) → voice-audit (output scan) → emotional-hook-test (feeling-level gate). All three should inform content review before publishing.

cognitive-memory.description: Cross-repository cognitive memory system. Persists knowledge across sessions using remember, recall, tree, extract, and forget operations. Memory store at ~/.memory/memory.json. Not a database — a reasoning layer that encodes selectively, consolidates contradictions, and forgets intentionally.

autoLoad.note: Loaded via prompt keywords or session-start event. Not loaded on file edits.

cognitive-memory.activationMessage: Cognitive memory activated. Use remember/recall/tree/forget via: python3 /home/user/.memory/memory_ops.py <command>

performance-analyzer.description: Web performance optimization (Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse) for cruise planning site

ship-page-validator.description: Auto-validates ship pages against SHIP_PAGE_CHECKLIST_v3.010 standards after creation or modification. Checks theological foundation, AI-breadcrumbs, ICP-Lite, JSON-LD, WCAG accessibility, and all required content sections.

postWriteAction.description: Auto-validate ship page against v3.010 checklist

ship-page-validator.activationMessage: 🚢 Ship page validator activated. Will auto-check compliance after modifications.

must_have: AI-breadcrumbs with entity, type, parent, category
ICP-Lite v1.4 meta tags (ai-summary, last-reviewed, content-protocol)
7 JSON-LD blocks (Organization, WebSite, BreadcrumbList, Review, Person, WebPage, FAQPage)
WCAG accessibility (skip link, ARIA live regions, landmark roles)
All required content sections (First Look, Stats, Dining, Logbook, Videos, Deck Plans, Live Tracker, FAQ, Attribution)
NO placeholder images (BLOCKING ERROR — omit image or hide container if no real image available)

investigate.description: Deep multi-LLM research pipeline that produces validated ship and port pages. 4 phases: reconnaissance → triage → deep research → synthesis → page generation.
investigate.activationMessage: Investigation pipeline ready. Specify a ship name or port to research and build a validated page.

coActivation.note: Investigation output feeds into page generation. Ship-page-validator fires automatically on ship page writes. Voice hooks enforce Like-a-human on all content.

orchestrate.description: Full multi-LLM pipeline orchestration. Runs the cruising pipeline (Read Standards → Generate → Content Review → Completeness Check → UX Challenge → Integrate) coordinating GPT, Gemini, and Grok as consultants while Claude remains lead developer.
orchestrate.activationMessage: Orchestrator active. Claude leads — external models consult.

coActivation.note: Orchestration output feeds into page generation pipeline. All standards enforced on integrated output.

consult.description: Quick multi-LLM second opinion. Sends a single prompt to GPT, Gemini, or Grok with a role-based system prompt and returns structured feedback.
consult.activationMessage: Consultation ready. One model, one question, structured feedback.

coActivation.note: All consultation feedback must be verified before incorporation.

careful-not-clever.description: Integrity guardrail: enforces careful, verified, documented work over clever shortcuts. Two layers: (1) Process discipline — read before editing, verify, document. (2) Technical discipline — semantic equivalence, pattern verification, pre-commit checks. Both fire on every write via session-start-guardrail.sh.

autoLoad.note: Process guardrail (mainFile) + Technical guardrail (supplementaryFile) both injected via session-start-guardrail.sh on every prompt

careful-not-clever.activationMessage: Careful, not clever. Both guardrails active: process discipline + technical verification.

before_modify: Have you READ this file in the current session?
Have you checked for class name / reference conflicts?
Have you stated and verified your assumptions?
during_modify: Are you updating tracking files alongside the work (not after)?
Are you making one logical change at a time?
After bulk changes, did you spot-check 2-3 files?
after_modify: Is every claim in your commit message verifiable?
Did you update all cross-references (plan files, metrics, tracking docs)?
Would the user trust this work if they checked every file?
must_reject: Editing files without reading them first
Assuming class names are unique without grepping
Making improvements the user didn't ask for
Silently skipping problems instead of reporting them
Updating metrics in one file but not others
Using placeholder images on ANY page (BLOCKING ERROR — no exceptions)
guardrails.philosophy: Integrity over speed. Verified over assumed. Documented over remembered. Careful over clever.

venue-page-writer.description: Researches and writes authentic logbook entries for cruise venue pages. Handles review research, negative flips, fact verification, voice consistency across 5 cruise lines. Uses web search + Gemini for research, with authenticity stress testing.
venue-page-writer.activationMessage: Venue page writer active. Research real reviews, write authentic logbooks, verify every fact.

coActivation.note: Logbook writing fires careful-not-clever (process) + content-strategy/Like-a-human (voice). Voice-audit fires before commit.

icp-2.description: ICP-2 — Human-First SEO & AEO standard for 2026. Supersedes ICP-Lite v1.4 and seo-optimizer. Blocks HTML edits that don't meet ICP-2 compliance.
icp-2.activationMessage: ICP-2 guardrail active. HTML edits will be validated for SEO/AEO compliance. Non-compliance is a blocking error.

notes.project: In the Wake - Cruise planning site (merged with FOM enhancements)

hook_wiring.UserPromptSubmit: session-start-guardrail.sh → injects BOTH Careful files (process v1.0 + technical root)
PreToolUse_Bash: voice-audit-hook.sh → injects voice-audit diagnostic before git commit when content files are staged
PostToolUse_Edit_Write: port-content-voice-hook.sh → injects Like-a-human v2.0.0 voice guide for ALL content writes

activation_matrix.pre_commit_content: voice-audit + emotional-hook-test (fire before git commit when content files are staged)

priority_order: 1. AI-First: Structure content so AI can accurately understand
3. Google Second: SEO is tertiary, not primary

guardrail_principles.seo: SEO skills must focus on technical SEO (schema.org, semantic HTML, meta tags) that benefits AI + humans + search engines simultaneously. Reject keyword stuffing, readability compromises, or removal of AI-helpful elements.
guardrail_principles.content: Content skills must prioritize authentic, natural language for humans while maintaining AI comprehension through structure. Reject robotic, keyword-optimized copy.
guardrail_principles.accessibility: Accessibility benefits all audiences (humans, AI, assistive tech, search engines). Always prioritize.
guardrail_principles.performance: Performance improves human experience and supports all other goals. Always prioritize.
guardrail_principles.theological: Faith-scented reflections are core to CITW identity. Never compromise theological foundation for secular optimization.

itw_lite_philosophy.skill_filtering: All skill suggestions must pass through ITW-Lite lens: Does it help AI? Does it maintain/improve human experience? Does it happen to help SEO? (Bonus!) Does it sacrifice human/AI for SEO? (Reject!)

enforcement_types.suggest: Skill suggestion appears but doesn't block execution
enforcement_types.block: Requires skill to be used before proceeding (guardrail)

priority_levels.low: Optional - Trigger only for explicit matches
